2605.01374.title: MTA: Multi-Granular Trajectory Alignment for Large Language Model Distillation
2605.01374.paper_summary: Knowledge distillation is a key technique for compressing large language models (LLMs), but most existing methods align representations at fixed layers or token-level outputs, ignoring how representations evolve across depth. As a result, the student is only weakly guided to capture the teacher's internal relational structure during distillation, which limits knowledge transfer. To address this limitation, we propose Multi-Granular Trajectory Alignment (MTA), a framework that aligns teacher and student representations along their layer-wise transformation trajectory. MTA adopts a layer-adaptive strategy: lower layers are aligned at the word level to preserve lexical information, while higher layers operate on phrase-level spans (e.g., noun and verb phrases) to capture compositional semantics. We instantiate this idea through a Dynamic Structural Alignment loss that matches the relative geometry among semantic units within each layer. This design is motivated by empirical findings that Transformer representations become increasingly abstract with depth, and is also consistent with linguistic views in which higher-level meaning emerges through the composition of lower-level lexical units. We further incorporate a Hidden Representation Alignment loss to directly align selected teacher-student layers. Experiments show that MTA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on standard benchmarks, with ablations confirming the contribution of each component.
2605.01374.paper_summary_zh: 知識蒸餾是一種壓縮大型語言模型（LLMs）的關鍵技術，但大多數現有方法在固定層或標記級輸出上對齊表示，忽略了表示在深度上的演變。因此，學生在蒸餾過程中僅受到微弱的指導以捕捉教師的內部關係結構，這限制了知識轉移。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了多粒度軌跡對齊（MTA），這是一個沿著層級轉換軌跡對齊教師和學生表示的框架。MTA採用層自適應策略：較低層在詞級別對齊以保留詞彙信息，而較高層則在短語級範圍內操作（例如，名詞短語和動詞短語）以捕捉組合語義。我們通過動態結構對齊損失來實現這一想法，該損失匹配每層內語義單元之間的相對幾何。這一設計受到實證研究結果的啟發，該結果顯示Transformer表示隨著深度的增加而變得越來越抽象，並且與語言學觀點一致，即高層次的意義通過低層次詞彙單元的組合而出現。我們進一步納入了一個隱藏表示對齊損失，以直接對齊選定的教師-學生層。實驗顯示，MTA在標準基準上穩定地超越了最先進的基線，消融實驗確認了每個組件的貢獻。
2605.01374.author: Pham Khanh Chi et.al.
2605.01374.authors: Pham Khanh Chi, Quoc Phong Dao, Thuat Nguyen, Linh Ngo Van, Trung Le, Thanh Hong Nguyen
